title: Deep learning of causal structures in high dimensions under data limitations
data_sources: Kemmeren et al. yeast gene deletion (Cell 2014)

data_scope.yeast_genes_full_genome: p = 5,535 genes (full yeast genome); n = 706; m = 753
data_scope.human_k562_genes: p = 8,552 genes (K562 leukaemia cell line); single-cell CRISPR
data_scope.human_rpe_genes: p = 8,833 genes (RPE retinal cells); single-cell CRISPR
data_scope.simulation_snr_range: varying SNR; linear and nonlinear SEMs

core_conclusion: D2CL (deep discriminative causal learning) combines CNN and GNN towers within a causal risk framework to learn directed causal graphs across thousands of variables from limited, noisy, high-dimensional data. Validated on yeast (up to p=5,535 genes) and single-cell CRISPR human data (up to p=8,833 genes), D2CL outperforms all competitors and scales to p=50,000 nodes, demonstrating that deep learning can recover causal networks at genome scale where classical causal methods fail entirely.
why_it_matters: Achieves causal discovery (not just correlation) at genome scale under data poverty; integrates prior knowledge as training signal to generalize to unseen interventions; validated against real unseen laboratory experiments; tolerant to corrupted priors; unlocks disease-context-specific causal network inference for drug target identification.
why_nature: Step-change scalable solution to a frontier grand challenge (causal discovery at genome scale); novel causal risk framework with formal theoretical grounding (MGA, DCSI); biomedical translational framing (personalized medicine, drug response); reproducibility via Code Ocean compute capsule.
added_value_to_my_research: Transfer D2CL's causal risk framework from gene networks to spatiotemporal urban health networks — replacing associative MGWR with directed causal discovery for health disparity analysis
Replace Pearson/Lasso initial graph prior with a spatially-aware or flow-network prior to improve GNN tower performance in geospatial causal applications
Couple D2CL outputs with active learning to select which spatial observations to collect next — principled data collection for multi-source urban health fusion
Use D2CL's causal vs. predictive AUC gap as a diagnostic filter on SHAP attributions — yielding causal explainability for my spatial ML models rather than confounded feature importance
Extend D2CL with context-conditioned causal graphs across population subgroups — grounding 2SFCA catchment delineation in learned subgroup-specific causal access-health relationships

related.title: Causal inference and counterfactual prediction in machine learning for actionable healthcare
related.relation: causal inference framework for healthcare outcomes — motivates health-geography application of D2CL

related.title: Stable learning establishes some common ground between causal inference and machine learning
related.relation: stable learning bridges causal inference and ML — relevant to replacing Pearson/Lasso graph priors

related.title: Active learning for optimal intervention design in causal models
related.relation: active selection of causal interventions — direct complement to D2CL's passive prior-knowledge input

related.title: Improving generalization of machine learning-identified biomarkers using causal modelling with examples from immune receptor diagnostics
related.relation: causal modelling to avoid spurious biomarkers — methodological parallel for auditing confounded spatial ML features

related.title: When causal inference meets deep learning
related.relation: Bayesian network + deep learning for causal structure learning — conceptual precursor and comparison point for D2CL

figures.caption: Conceptual overview of D2CL — abstract and biological application workflows showing how prior knowledge and data combine to yield a global causal graph
D2CL architecture — CNN tower (KDE image inputs) and GNN tower (subgraph embeddings) fused to output directed edge probabilities
Simulation benchmark results — ROC curves and AUC across noise levels, linear/nonlinear SEMs at p=1,500 nodes
Yeast gene deletion results — performance as a function of interventions m, sample size n, and full-genome dimension p=5,535; CNN vs. GNN vs. full D2CL ablation
Robustness to corrupted prior knowledge (10% label errors) and causal direction analysis — CNN feature maps differentiate A->B from B->A
Human CRISPR results on K562 and RPE cells (p > 8,000 genes); competing methods cannot scale to this regime; runtime comparison
